description: Anchor case: H-4 EAD timing is an operational-reality claim. Official USCIS SLA describes the target, not the outcome. Practitioner/anecdotal evidence should be primary.

request.message: How long is H-4 and H-4 EAD currently taking when filed with H-1B renewal on premium processing?

unacceptable_failure_modes: cites only official USCIS SLA as the answer to how long it takes
presents USCIS 15-day premium SLA as the H-4 EAD processing time
no practitioner or forum evidence in the final answer
expected.notes: The correct answer cites recent practitioner reports (e.g., 4-8 months) as the primary figure, with the official SLA given as context only.

description: Pure policy claim: H-1B cap and lottery rules are official policy. Anecdotes about lottery outcomes should not override official rules as the primary answer.

request.message: What is the annual H-1B visa cap and how does the lottery selection work?

unacceptable_failure_modes: presents individual lottery anecdotes as the primary explanation of the cap rules
does not cite official USCIS source for the cap number
cites only forum discussion instead of official policy

description: Anecdotal evidence exists but is noisy/outlier — one extreme case. System should hedge, cite consensus, and not adopt a single outlier as the primary answer.

request.message: How long does PERM labor certification typically take right now?

unacceptable_failure_modes: adopts a single anecdote (e.g. approved in 3 months) as the definitive answer without disclosing that it is an outlier
presents a single forum post as consensus
no hedging or range when variance is known to be high
expected.notes: The answer should give a range reflecting practitioner consensus, not elevate one data point.

description: Anecdotal evidence is the only available signal (no official timeline data exists). It should become primary by necessity, not be suppressed for lacking an official source.

request.message: What is the real-world experience of getting an I-485 interview waived at a USCIS field office in 2025?

unacceptable_failure_modes: refuses to answer or heavily disclaims because only anecdotal evidence is available
surfaces no practitioner experience because official data is absent
labels the answer unusable because it lacks official sources
expected.notes: Anecdotal evidence from multiple independent sources IS the best available signal here.

description: Official source says X (SLA / target), recent practitioner reports say Y (actual outcome). Both must appear in the answer, not silently blended.

request.message: How long does a National Interest Waiver (NIW) I-140 take to get approved after filing?

unacceptable_failure_modes: blends official processing time estimate and practitioner wait times into one hedged claim without naming the disagreement
cites only USCIS published processing times without noting whether practitioners report different outcomes
presents official estimate as the definitive answer
expected.notes: Both the official estimate and practitioner reports should be stated explicitly as distinct positions.

description: A high-authority source (e.g. law firm site) has a 2021 processing time for a volatile immigration queue. It should be downweighted or flagged as potentially stale for an operational claim.

request.message: What is the current processing time for a green card through marriage to a US citizen?

unacceptable_failure_modes: cites a 2021 or 2022 processing time as current without flagging staleness
presents outdated practitioner data from 2+ years ago as the current state
does not note that USCIS processing times change frequently
expected.notes: Fresh evidence (2024-2025) should be preferred; stale evidence should be flagged as historical or downweighted.

description: Official USCIS policy page with no visible 'last updated' date. Topic is policy (not operational timing). Unknown date must NOT cause it to be downranked as stale — policy from an authoritative source without a date is still admissible.

request.message: What are the eligibility requirements for H-4 EAD authorization?

unacceptable_failure_modes: refuses to use or heavily discounts an official USCIS page because it lacks a visible publication date
treats unknown date as equivalent to stale date for a policy claim
omits official policy details because the official source has no timestamp
expected.notes: Unknown date + official domain = admissible for policy claims. Only known-old + current-operational = penalized.

description: Simple, low-stakes current fact: the current USCIS filing fee for an I-765. Expect a quick, cited answer from an official source.

request.message: What is the current USCIS filing fee for Form I-765?

unacceptable_failure_modes: gives a fee from an outdated fee schedule without noting it may have changed
no citation to USCIS official source
expected.notes: This is a quick-path, low-depth case. Over-engineering is not expected.

description: The same article content about a processing time is reposted/syndicated on two different domains. The system must not count them as 2 independent corroborating sources.

request.message: What are practitioners currently reporting about O-1A visa processing times in 2025?

unacceptable_failure_modes: counts two copies of the same syndicated article on different domains as two independent data points
claims '3 independent sources confirm X' when two are from the same original content
expected.notes: The independence proxy (source_family + content_fingerprint) should detect same-content reposts and prevent double-counting.

description: Current reported side effects of a recently approved drug. Both FDA labeling (official_policy) and post-market patient reports (operational_reality/anecdotal_case) are valid. Neither should be silenced.

request.message: What side effects are patients currently reporting for tirzepatide (Zepbound/Mounjaro) in real-world use?

unacceptable_failure_modes: cites only the FDA label as the answer to real-world patient experience
suppresses patient forums/reports because they are anecdotal
does not distinguish between label side effects and post-market reports

description: Current earnings timeline for a public company. Official filings are official_policy; analyst commentary is expert_interpretation. Investor community reports of forward guidance reactions are operational_reality.

request.message: When is Tesla expected to report Q2 2025 earnings and what are analysts currently expecting?

unacceptable_failure_modes: conflates official filing schedule with analyst consensus estimates without distinguishing them
presents a single analyst estimate as consensus without noting range

description: Current capability claim for a software product that ships frequently. Official docs may lag latest release. User reports / changelogs are operational_reality.

request.message: Does Claude claude-opus-4 currently support tool use / function calling via the API?

unacceptable_failure_modes: contradicts official Anthropic documentation by relying solely on third-party blog post
does not cite official API docs at all
presents stale API capability info (from 2023) as current
expected.notes: Official docs are the right primary source for product capabilities.

description: Operational experience with a tech product at scale — latency, reliability, real-world cost. Official benchmarks (official_policy) are targets; practitioner reports are operational_reality.

request.message: What latency and reliability are developers actually experiencing with the OpenAI GPT-4o API in production in 2025?

unacceptable_failure_modes: cites only OpenAI SLA or benchmark as the answer to real-world latency
presents no practitioner/developer reports
treats all latency claims as equally reliable regardless of source

description: Medical guidance where official guidelines differ from emerging research or recent practitioner experience. Both must be stated explicitly.

request.message: What does current evidence say about the effectiveness of continuous glucose monitors for non-diabetic people trying to optimize metabolism?

unacceptable_failure_modes: presents only official clinical guidelines without noting the emerging practitioner/research landscape
presents only anecdotal wellness influencer reports without noting the lack of RCT evidence
blends official guidance and emerging research into one hedged statement without labeling the source of each
expected.notes: The answer must distinguish: official clinical guidelines, academic RCT evidence, and real-world practitioner/biohacker reports — stating each as a distinct position.

description: Phase 6 anchor case: a 5-entity comparison request where the planner must guarantee per-entity dedicated evidence, not just broad overview queries that happen to name-check all five. Caught a real production failure where 2 of 5 frameworks got 'no evidence' placeholders.

request.message: Research the top 5 agentic AI frameworks in 2025: LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, Haystack, and LlamaIndex Workflows. Provide for each: architecture model, multi-agent coordination approach, production readiness, and known failure modes. Then synthesize a recommendation for the best framework for an enterprise orchestration layer and explain why.

unacceptable_failure_modes: 2 or more of the 5 named frameworks receive 'no evidence found in retrieved sources' or 'not described in evidence' for every dimension
answer has a complete section for LangGraph and CrewAI but only a placeholder or single sentence for Haystack or LlamaIndex Workflows
evidence pack contains zero items with source_url matching haystack or llamaindex domains
recommendation section omits one or more of the 5 named frameworks entirely
expected.notes: The planner must emit at least one per-entity dedicated query for each named framework (Phase 6.1). The coverage contract must have per-(framework, dimension) cells so open_cells() reflects per-framework gaps (Phase 6.2). Breadth-first follow-up must fill all 5 frameworks before deepening any (Phase 6.3).

description: Phase 6 anchor case: a comparison request where one named entity (AutoGen) has had a significant status change (moved to maintenance mode, Microsoft Agent Framework announced as GA successor). The system must surface this in the main recommendation, not as a footnote. Caught a real production failure.

request.message: Compare AutoGen, LangGraph, and CrewAI as multi-agent orchestration frameworks for enterprise production use in 2025. Which would you recommend and why?

unacceptable_failure_modes: evaluates AutoGen as an actively developed framework without surfacing that Microsoft moved it to maintenance mode
recommends AutoGen without noting the Microsoft Agent Framework (AG2/0.4) transition
recommendation section treats all three frameworks as equivalently current without surfacing the AutoGen lifecycle status
buries the AutoGen deprecation/successor information in a footnote or validation note rather than in the main recommendation
expected.notes: The planner must run a status-check query for each named entity (Phase 6.4). A hit on AutoGen maintenance/deprecation must surface in the recommendation as a primary signal, not a footnote — the Phase 2 synthesis instruction + claim_role=official_policy should ensure this.
